methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Xcimer Energy is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Conner Galloway and Alexander Valys, having raised over $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of four private companies at the same stage and sector—Watershed, Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy—alongside one public peer (LNZA), providing a reasonably tight reference set for frontier-tech energy infrastructure.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression anchors to the $100M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $200M. Peer-median analysis across the four same-stage, same-sector comparables produces a median valuation of $175M, with a range of $110M to $1.8B; the wide spread reflects heterogeneity in late-stage outliers like Watershed at $1.8B versus earlier-stage anchors. These methods triangulate to a median of $188M, with a defensible range of $149M to $230M.

The analysis supports a valuation in the $175M–$200M band as most probable. Key uncertainties include how Xcimer's technical progress and go-to-market traction compare to peer trajectories, particularly relative to Watershed and Graphyte at the upper end, and whether the $100M+ raised reflects recent post-money or is substantially older. The 2.0x stage multiplier is standard but not invariant and may compress or expand depending on market conditions and fundraising velocity.
